model.description: MiMo • The flagship foundation for the agentic era, built for complex, long-horizon, multi-step agentic tasks.

pick.rationale: Michigan holds significant advantages as the #1 overall seed with elite metrics on both ends of the floor (KenPom/Torvik rank #2, offense rank 7-8, defense rank 2), while Alabama's elite offense (rank 3) is severely hampered by the loss of star guard Aden Holloway due to arrest. The Wolverines' superior defensive efficiency should contain Alabama's attack, and their balanced scoring can exploit Alabama's weak defense (rank 67). Despite Michigan's minor injury concerns with Yaxel Lendeborg's ankle, the Crimson Tide's off-court turmoil and defensive vulnerabilities make Michigan the clear favorite to advance.

reasoningStep.title: Sweet 16 Midwest: Michigan vs Alabama Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Michigan's elite two-way profile and Alabama's significant personnel loss create a clear advantage for the Wolverines.
evidence: Michigan ranks #2 in both KenPom and Torvik with a 31-2 record, featuring top-10 offense (rank 7-8) and top-2 defense.
Alabama ranks #16-17 with a 23-9 record, featuring elite offense (rank 3) but poor defense (rank 67).
Alabama is without star guard Aden Holloway due to felony drug arrest, significantly weakening their backcourt depth and offensive firepower.
Michigan's Yaxel Lendeborg is expected to play through a low-ankle sprain, limiting his effectiveness but not removing him from the lineup.
The matchup pits Michigan's elite defense against Alabama's elite offense, but Alabama's defensive weaknesses create a significant mismatch for Michigan's balanced attack.

result.text: Yaxel Lendeborg injury update for NCAA Tournament Skip to main content Home Schedule Football Basketball Recruiting Big Ten Sports Odds Newsletter College Sports Wire USA TODAY Sports Michigan's Yaxel Lendeborg ready for NCAA Tournament despite injury Isaiah Hole Wolverines Wire Near the end of the Big Ten Tournament championship game, while not the worst news in the world, it wasn't good news for the maize and blue: star forward Yaxel Lendeborg got rolled up on and ended the loss to Purdue with a low-ankle sprain. Timing-wise, it's not ideal for the Michigan basketball star, but at the same time, it could be worse, as he appears to be able to go in the NCAA Tournament, which starts on Tuesday. On Wednesday, as the Wolverines prepare to take on Howard in the Round of 64, Lendeborg should be ready to go, but at the same time, he isn't entering the tournament at 100%. "Yeah, I’m fine. I started moving, running around yesterday," Lendeborg said. "I had a little bit of issues cutting a little bit, but today I think I’m going to feel a lot better. "When I get into the game mindset, I think I’ll be all right." Having come from junior college and then UAB, this is the first time that Lendeborg is playing for a favorite in the NCAA Tournament. Because he got that earlier experience, he knows what it's like to be in Howard's shoes. But now that he is where he is, he's determined to suit up and do everything he can to ensure that the season goes on as long as it possibly can. "Everybody is a threat. For them to get here, they have to win the championship," Lendeborg said. "I’ve been in that predicament before when I was a mid major. We’re going to go in there with nothing to lose so we’re going to play our hearts out. And no matter what happens, we’re happy we made it. "Being on this side now, it’s the same thing Rez said, I don’t want to lose, and this is going to be my last college game ever, so I want to continue my streak." The Wolverines and Bison will tip off at 7:10 p.m. EDT, and the game will be broadcast on CBS. result.text: Yaxel Lendeborg injury update for NCAA Tournament Skip to main content Home Schedule Football Basketball Recruiting Big Ten Sports Odds Newsletter College Sports Wire USA TODAY Sports Michigan's Yaxel Lendeborg ready for NCAA Tournament despite injury Isaiah Hole Wolverines Wire Near the end of the Big Ten Tournament championship game, while not the worst news in the world, it wasn't good news for the maize and blue: star forward Yaxel Lendeborg got rolled up on and ended the loss to Purdue with a low-ankle sprain. Timing-wise, it's not ideal for the Michigan basketball star, but at the same time, it could be worse, as he appears to be able to go in the NCAA Tournament, which starts on Tuesday. On Wednesday, as the Wolverines prepare to take on Howard in the Round of 64, Lendeborg should be ready to go, but at the same time, he isn't entering the tournament at 100%. "Yeah, I’m fine. I started moving, running around yesterday," Lendeborg said. "I had a little bit of issues cutting a little bit, but today I think I’m going to feel a lot better. "When I get into the game mindset, I think I’ll be all right." Having come from junior college and then UAB, this is the first time that Lendeborg is playing for a favorite in the NCAA Tournament. Because he got that earlier experience, he knows what it's like to be in Howard's shoes. But now that he is where he is, he's determined to suit up and do everything he can to ensure that the season goes on as long as it possibly can. "Everybody is a threat. For them to get here, they have to win the championship," Lendeborg said. "I’ve been in that predicament before when I was a mid major. We’re going to go in there with nothing to lose so we’re going to play our hearts out. And no matter what happens, we’re happy we made it. "Being on this side now, it’s the same thing Rez said, I don’t want to lose, and this is going to be my last college game ever, so I want to continue my streak." The Wolverines and Bison will tip off at 7:10 p.m. EDT, and the game will be broadcast on CBS. This suggests Michigan has a significant edge on both ends, but Alabama's offense could keep them in the game. However, without Holloway, Alabama's backcourt depth is weakened.
